one_sentence_thesis: Negative feedback can create stable improvement beyond Positive-only learning, but historical negative actions can become learner-relative far field and drive the aggregate policy target toward a feasibility boundary; DRPO attenuates that far-field contribution while retaining useful local repulsion.
identity_statement: This is one DRPO manuscript being rewritten. Optimistic-DRO quality selection remains a self-contained appendix result, while exponential remoteness control is the main signed-update mechanism; neither is narrated as an old paper followed by a sequel.
merge_statement: The content baseline is the user-approved v0.9-review. Repository v0.9 and v0.9.1 contribute only the changes listed as accepted in the merge ledger.

gates.evidence: Scientific claims and experiment states remain governed by handoff and registry.
The approved v0.9-review is recorded as the content baseline rather than an inspiration for silent rewriting.
The merge ledger distinguishes review from rewrite and records every imported or rejected change.
Strategy, graph, outline, prose, and correction appendix encode one DRPO manuscript being rewritten.
The new merged artifact is versioned v0.9.2 rather than silently replacing v0.9 or v0.9.1.
The one-sentence contribution connects useful negative feedback, far-field amplification, equilibrium loss, and DRPO control.
The missing link is strict badness--distance or badness--rarity isolation.
The transferable principle is to control excessive learner-relative far-field negative influence while retaining local utility.
Theorem 1 has one job: stable extrapolation, boundary approach, and loss of finite equilibrium.
DRPO modifies the theorem-level aggregate negative contribution.
RQ3 requires direct measurement of raw and weighted aggregate negative influence.
Optimistic-DRO quality selection and exponential remoteness tapering are stated as distinct results; no hard-to-Exp derivation is invented.
Main prose removes anticipatory global-convergence and frozen-advantage disclaimers.
P03 names the strongest rival explanation and the decisive matched control.
Claim evidence matrix separates scientific status from execution state.
Negative, TBD, and failed results remain explicit and are assigned appendix or table slots.
Each RQ and graph node has a primary claim and evidence owner.
C-U1/D-U1 are controlled; Hopper/Countdown are external.
Paired seeds, common data, matched budgets, and best/terminal reporting are required.
Dynamic claims require terminal or continuation audit.
Task collapse, policy-family boundary, and NaN/Inf are separate fields.
The abstract follows resource, failure, theory, method, evidence, implication.
The Introduction uses six moves; P03 combines existing controls and matched isolation.
Related Work is organized by method lines with precise relationship statements.
Theory follows intuition, formal object, theorem, interpretation, predictions, and method bridge.
Method follows raw term, weighting, controlled term, proposition, and ablations.
Experiments use external occurrence, controlled explanation, phase/control, and external closure.
The appendix includes proofs, corrected Gaussian/categorical dynamics, protocols, results, reproducibility, Optimistic DRO, and correction ledger.
Every stable node has one claim and one reader question.
Paragraphs move from established context to new claim.
Claim verbs are calibrated through claim-evidence status records.
Prose uses concrete policy, update, sample, theorem, and method actors.
Scientific-completeness prose and compression are separate passes.
Figure 1 carries the useful-to-destructive arc and evidence architecture.
Each generated visual has one primary claim and is labeled schematic.
Tables encode environment roles, controlled comparisons, and external closure.
Captions state claim, comparison, status, and interpretation.
Current citations compile from the included bibliography; no placeholder citation is introduced.
The supplied ICML/Overleaf project compiles cleanly and a direct-upload ZIP is generated.
Stable-ID sync is bidirectional and same-node conflicts fail closed.
Semantic prose edits expose structured Claim, Reader question, Role, Logical moves, and Evidence fields.
Outline, blueprint, prose, TeX, appendix, figures, PDF, and Overleaf ZIP are reproducible graph projections.

minor: C-U1 full-parameter distance-only attribution still requires the registered same-state/same-ray or Jacobian-gain closure before the strongest source claim is activated.
D-U1 paper-facing common/rare isolation still requires the registered semantic, quality, count, and coefficient audit.
Hopper and Countdown quantitative prose remains TBD until formal terminal-audited runs are complete.
The schematic figures must be replaced or supplemented with formal empirical plots once the corresponding evidence is frozen.
The first generated prose is a scientific-completeness draft and still requires user content review before canonical activation.

unresolved_experiment_dependencies.dependency: C-U1 same-state/same-ray radial probe or registered Jacobian-gain decomposition
formal Hopper and Countdown terminal-audited runs

generated_outputs.validation: exists, non-empty, compiles without critical LaTeX warnings, visually rendered
exists, non-empty, direct-upload package excludes build intermediates and legacy_source
